revision.text:
'''I Hakase''' (еҢ»еҚҡеЈ«) was an official position under the {{ill|RitsuryЕҚ system|ja|еҫӢд»ӨеҲ¶}}, belonging to the {{ill|Bureau of Pharmacy|ja|е…ёи–¬еҜ®}} within the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ (еҫӢд»ӨеҲ¶)}}.
== Overview ==
The term "I Hakase" first appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ}} in entries for the 14th and 15th years of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|ж¬ҪжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. These references, however, pertain to doctors sent as tribute from {{ill|Baekje|ja|зҷҫжёҲ}} and are unrelated to Japan's own administrative system. By the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ}} ({{ill|675|ja|675е№ҙ}}), the term "Outer Pharmacy Bureau" (еӨ–и–¬еҜ®), later known as the Bureau of Medicine, is documented. It is inferred that the administrative structure equivalent to the Bureau of Medicine had already been established during the reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress JitЕҚ.
The position of I Hakase was held by a single appointee with an equivalent rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|жӯЈдёғдҪҚдёӢ}}, making it the highest-ranking post among the various specialists within the Bureau of Medicine. Among the ten physicians employed, one with superior knowledge and experience was selected as I Hakase. The primary duties involved teaching various {{ill|medical prescriptions|ja|и–¬ж–№}} (yakuhЕҚ) and {{ill|pulse diagnosis texts|ja|и„ҲзөҢ}} (myakugyЕҚ) to the medical practitioners (еҢ»з”ҹ). Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.
In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, {{ill|791|ja|е»¶жҡҰ10е№ҙ}}, the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>
== References == {{и„ҡжіЁгғҳгғ«гғ—}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "I Hakase," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|еӣҪеҸІеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Volume 1, p. 769, by Imae Hiromichi, Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan, 1979.
== See Also == * {{ill|IdЕҚ|ja|еҢ»йҒ“}} * {{ill|I TokugyЕҚsei|ja|еҢ»еҫ—жҘӯз”ҹ}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Medical-stub}}

'''I Hakase''' (еҢ»еҚҡеЈ«) was an official position under the {{ill|RitsuryЕҚ system|ja|еҫӢд»ӨеҲ¶}}, belonging to the {{ill|Bureau of Pharmacy|ja|е…ёи–¬еҜ®}} within the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ (еҫӢд»ӨеҲ¶)}}.
== Overview ==
The term "I Hakase" first appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ}} in entries for the 14th and 15th years of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|ж¬ҪжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. These references, however, pertain to doctors sent as tribute from {{ill|Baekje|ja|зҷҫжёҲ}} and are unrelated to Japan's own administrative system. By the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ}} ({{ill|675|ja|675е№ҙ}}), the term "Outer Pharmacy Bureau" (еӨ–и–¬еҜ®), later known as the Bureau of Medicine, is documented. It is inferred that the administrative structure equivalent to the Bureau of Medicine had already been established during the reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress JitЕҚ.
The position of I Hakase was held by a single appointee with an equivalent rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|жӯЈдёғдҪҚдёӢ}}, making it the highest-ranking post among the various specialists within the Bureau of Medicine. Among the ten physicians employed, one with superior knowledge and experience was selected as I Hakase. The primary duties involved teaching various {{ill|medical prescriptions|ja|и–¬ж–№}} (yakuhЕҚ) and {{ill|pulse diagnosis texts|ja|и„ҲзөҢ}} (myakugyЕҚ) to the medical practitioners (еҢ»з”ҹ). Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.
In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, {{ill|791|ja|е»¶жҡҰ10е№ҙ}}, the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>
== References == {{и„ҡжіЁгғҳгғ«гғ—}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "I Hakase," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|еӣҪеҸІеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Volume 1, p. 769, by Imae Hiromichi, Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan, 1979.
== See Also == * {{ill|IdЕҚ|ja|еҢ»йҒ“}} * {{ill|I TokugyЕҚsei|ja|еҢ»еҫ—жҘӯз”ҹ}}

The position of I Hakase was held by a single appointee with an equivalent rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|жӯЈдёғдҪҚдёӢ}}, making it the highest-ranking post among the various specialists within the Bureau of Medicine. Among the ten physicians employed, one with superior knowledge and experience was selected as I Hakase. The primary duties involved teaching various {{ill|medical prescriptions|ja|и–¬ж–№}} (yakuhЕҚ) and {{ill|pulse diagnosis texts|ja|и„ҲзөҢ}} (myakugyЕҚ) to the medical practitioners (еҢ»з”ҹ). Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.
In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, {{ill|791|ja|е»¶жҡҰ10е№ҙ}}, the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>

According to a decree issued on February 18, {{ill|791|ja|е»¶жҡҰ10е№ҙ}}, the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>

'''I Hakase''' (еҢ»еҚҡеЈ«) was an official position under the {{ill|RitsuryЕҚ system|ja|еҫӢд»ӨеҲ¶}}, belonging to the {{ill|Bureau of Pharmacy|ja|е…ёи–¬еҜ®}} within the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ (еҫӢд»ӨеҲ¶)}}.
== Overview ==
The term "I Hakase" first appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ}} in entries for the 14th and 15th years of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|ж¬ҪжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. These references, however, pertain to doctors sent as tribute from {{ill|Baekje|ja|зҷҫжёҲ}} and are unrelated to Japan's own administrative system. By the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ}} ({{ill|675|ja|675е№ҙ}}), the term "Outer Pharmacy Bureau" (еӨ–и–¬еҜ®), later known as the Bureau of Medicine, is documented. It is inferred that the administrative structure equivalent to the Bureau of Medicine had already been established during the reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress JitЕҚ.
The position of I Hakase was held by a single appointee with an equivalent rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|жӯЈдёғдҪҚдёӢ}}, making it the highest-ranking post among the various specialists within the Bureau of Medicine. Among the ten physicians employed, one with superior knowledge and experience was selected as I Hakase. The primary duties involved teaching various {{ill|medical prescriptions|ja|и–¬ж–№}} (yakuhЕҚ) and {{ill|pulse diagnosis texts|ja|и„ҲзөҢ}} (myakugyЕҚ) to the medical practitioners (еҢ»з”ҹ). Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.
In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, {{ill|791|ja|е»¶жҡҰ10е№ҙ}}, the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>

'''I Hakase''' (еҢ»еҚҡеЈ«) was an official position under the {{ill|RitsuryЕҚ system|ja|еҫӢд»ӨеҲ¶}}, belonging to the {{ill|Bureau of Pharmacy|ja|е…ёи–¬еҜ®}} within the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ (еҫӢд»ӨеҲ¶)}}.
== Overview ==
The term "I Hakase" first appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ}} in entries for the 14th and 15th years of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|ж¬ҪжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. These references, however, pertain to doctors sent as tribute from {{ill|Baekje|ja|зҷҫжёҲ}} and are unrelated to Japan's own administrative system. By the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ}} ({{ill|675|ja|675е№ҙ}}), the term "Outer Pharmacy Bureau" (еӨ–и–¬еҜ®), later known as the Bureau of Medicine, is documented. It is inferred that the administrative structure equivalent to the Bureau of Medicine had already been established during the reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress JitЕҚ.
The position of I Hakase was held by a single appointee with an equivalent rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|жӯЈдёғдҪҚдёӢ|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, making it the highest-ranking post among the various specialists within the Bureau of Medicine. Among the ten physicians employed, one with superior knowledge and experience was selected as I Hakase. The primary duties involved teaching various {{ill|medical prescriptions|ja|и–¬ж–№}} (yakuhЕҚ) and {{ill|pulse diagnosis texts|ja|и„ҲзөҢ}} (myakugyЕҚ) to the medical practitioners (еҢ»з”ҹ). Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.
In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, {{ill|791|ja|е»¶жҡҰ10е№ҙ}}, the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>

These references, however, pertain to doctors sent as tribute from {{ill|Baekje|ja|зҷҫжёҲ}} and are unrelated to Japan's own administrative system. By the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ}} ({{ill|675|ja|675е№ҙ}}), the term "Outer Pharmacy Bureau" (еӨ–и–¬еҜ®), later known as the Bureau of Medicine, is documented. It is inferred that the administrative structure equivalent to the Bureau of Medicine had already been established during the reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress JitЕҚ.
The position of I Hakase was held by a single appointee with an equivalent rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|жӯЈдёғдҪҚдёӢ|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, making it the highest-ranking post among the various specialists within the Bureau of Medicine. Among the ten physicians employed, one with superior knowledge and experience was selected as I Hakase. The primary duties involved teaching various {{ill|medical prescriptions|ja|и–¬ж–№}} (yakuhЕҚ) and {{ill|pulse diagnosis texts|ja|и„ҲзөҢ}} (myakugyЕҚ) to the medical practitioners (еҢ»з”ҹ). Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.
In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, {{ill|791|ja|е»¶жҡҰ10е№ҙ}}, the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>

revision.text:
'''I Hakase''' (еҢ»еҚҡеЈ«) was an official position under the {{ill|RitsuryЕҚ system|ja|еҫӢд»ӨеҲ¶}}, belonging to the {{ill|Bureau of Pharmacy|ja|е…ёи–¬еҜ®}} within the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ (еҫӢд»ӨеҲ¶)}}.
== Overview ==
The term "I Hakase" first appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ}} in entries for the 14th and 15th years of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|ж¬ҪжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. These references, however, pertain to doctors sent as tribute from {{ill|Baekje|ja|зҷҫжёҲ}} and are unrelated to Japan's own administrative system. By the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|675|ja|675е№ҙ}}), the term "Outer Pharmacy Bureau" (еӨ–и–¬еҜ®), later known as the Bureau of Medicine, is documented. It is inferred that the administrative structure equivalent to the Bureau of Medicine had already been established during the reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress JitЕҚ.
The position of I Hakase was held by a single appointee with an equivalent rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|жӯЈдёғдҪҚдёӢ|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, making it the highest-ranking post among the various specialists within the Bureau of Medicine. Among the ten physicians employed, one with superior knowledge and experience was selected as I Hakase. The primary duties involved teaching various {{ill|medical prescriptions|ja|и–¬ж–№}} (yakuhЕҚ) and {{ill|pulse diagnosis texts|ja|и„ҲзөҢ}} (myakugyЕҚ) to the medical practitioners (еҢ»з”ҹ). Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.
In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, {{ill|791|ja|е»¶жҡҰ10е№ҙ}}, the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>
== References == {{и„ҡжіЁгғҳгғ«гғ—}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "I Hakase," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|еӣҪеҸІеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Volume 1, p. 769, by Imae Hiromichi, Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan, 1979.
== See Also == * {{ill|IdЕҚ|ja|еҢ»йҒ“}} * {{ill|I TokugyЕҚsei|ja|еҢ»еҫ—жҘӯз”ҹ}}

The term "I Hakase" first appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ}} in entries for the 14th and 15th years of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|ж¬ҪжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. These references, however, pertain to doctors sent as tribute from {{ill|Baekje|ja|зҷҫжёҲ}} and are unrelated to Japan's own administrative system. By the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|675|ja|675е№ҙ}}), the term "Outer Pharmacy Bureau" (еӨ–и–¬еҜ®), later known as the Bureau of Medicine, is documented. It is inferred that the administrative structure equivalent to the Bureau of Medicine had already been established during the reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress JitЕҚ.
The position of I Hakase was held by a single appointee with an equivalent rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|жӯЈдёғдҪҚдёӢ|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, making it the highest-ranking post among the various specialists within the Bureau of Medicine. The primary duties involved teaching various {{ill|medical prescriptions|ja|и–¬ж–№}} (yakuhЕҚ) and {{ill|pulse diagnosis texts|ja|и„ҲзөҢ}} (myakugyЕҚ) to the medical practitioners (еҢ»з”ҹ). Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.
In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, {{ill|791|ja|е»¶жҡҰ10е№ҙ}}, the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>

'''I Hakase''' (еҢ»еҚҡеЈ«) was an official position under the {{ill|RitsuryЕҚ system|ja|еҫӢд»ӨеҲ¶}}, belonging to the {{ill|Bureau of Pharmacy|ja|е…ёи–¬еҜ®}} within the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ (еҫӢд»ӨеҲ¶)}}.
== Overview ==
The term "I Hakase" first appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ}} in entries for the 14th and 15th years of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|ж¬ҪжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ}}. These references, however, pertain to doctors sent as tribute from {{ill|Baekje|ja|зҷҫжёҲ}} and are unrelated to Japan's own administrative system. By the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|675|ja|675е№ҙ}}), the term "Outer Pharmacy Bureau" (еӨ–и–¬еҜ®), later known as the Bureau of Medicine, is documented. It is inferred that the administrative structure equivalent to the Bureau of Medicine had already been established during the reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress JitЕҚ.
The position of I Hakase was held by a single appointee with an equivalent rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|жӯЈдёғдҪҚдёӢ|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, making it the highest-ranking post among the various specialists within the Bureau of Medicine. Among the ten physicians employed, one with superior knowledge and experience was selected as I Hakase. The primary duties involved teaching various {{ill|medical prescriptions|ja|и–¬ж–№}} (yakuhЕҚ) and {{ill|pulse diagnosis texts|ja|и„ҲзөҢ}} (myakugyЕҚ) to the medical practitioners (еҢ»з”ҹ). Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.
In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, {{ill|791|ja|е»¶жҡҰ10е№ҙ}}, the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "I Hakase," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|еӣҪеҸІеӨ§иҫһе…ё}}, Volume 1, p. 769, by Imae Hiromichi, Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan, 1979.
== See Also == * {{ill|IdЕҚ|ja|еҢ»йҒ“}} * {{ill|I TokugyЕҚsei|ja|еҢ»еҫ—жҘӯз”ҹ}}

The position of I Hakase was held by a single appointee with an equivalent rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|жӯЈдёғдҪҚдёӢ|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}, making it the highest-ranking post among the various specialists within the Bureau of Medicine. Among the ten physicians employed, one with superior knowledge and experience was selected as I Hakase. The primary duties involved teaching various {{ill|medical prescriptions|ja|и–¬ж–№|12=draft}} (yakuhЕҚ) and {{ill|pulse diagnosis texts|ja|и„ҲзөҢ|12=draft}} (myakugyЕҚ) to the medical practitioners (еҢ»з”ҹ). Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«|12=draft}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.
In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„|12=draft}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„|12=draft}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, {{ill|791|ja|е»¶жҡҰ10th year|12=draft}}, the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°|12=draft}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј|12=draft}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>

{{afc comment|1=I Hakase is too ambiguous in english so using Medical Hakase as the name[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 19:15, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''I Hakase''' (еҢ»еҚҡеЈ«) was an official position under the {{ill|RitsuryЕҚ system|ja|еҫӢд»ӨеҲ¶|12=draft}}, belonging to the {{ill|Bureau of Pharmacy|ja|е…ёи–¬еҜ®|12=draft}} within the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ (еҫӢд»ӨеҲ¶)|12=draft}}.
== Overview ==
The term "I Hakase" first appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ|12=draft}} in entries for the 14th and 15th years of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|ж¬ҪжҳҺеӨ©зҡҮ|12=draft}}. These references, however, pertain to doctors sent as tribute from {{ill|Baekje|ja|зҷҫжёҲ|12=draft}} and are unrelated to Japan's own administrative system. By the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} ({{ill|675|ja|675th year|12=draft}}), the term "Outer Pharmacy Bureau" (еӨ–и–¬еҜ®), later known as the Bureau of Medicine, is documented. It is inferred that the administrative structure equivalent to the Bureau of Medicine had already been established during the reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress JitЕҚ.
The position of I Hakase was held by a single appointee with an equivalent rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|жӯЈдёғдҪҚдёӢ|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}, making it the highest-ranking post among the various specialists within the Bureau of Medicine. Among the ten physicians employed, one with superior knowledge and experience was selected as I Hakase. The primary duties involved teaching various {{ill|medical prescriptions|ja|и–¬ж–№|12=draft}} (yakuhЕҚ) and {{ill|pulse diagnosis texts|ja|и„ҲзөҢ|12=draft}} (myakugyЕҚ) to the medical practitioners (еҢ»з”ҹ). Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«|12=draft}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.
In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„|12=draft}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„|12=draft}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, {{ill|791|ja|е»¶жҡҰ10th year|12=draft}}, the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°|12=draft}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј|12=draft}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "I Hakase," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|еӣҪеҸІеӨ§иҫһе…ё|12=draft}}, Volume 1, p. 769, by Imae Hiromichi, Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan, 1979.
== See Also == * {{ill|IdЕҚ|ja|еҢ»йҒ“|12=draft}} * {{ill|I TokugyЕҚsei|ja|еҢ»еҫ—жҘӯз”ҹ|12=draft}}

'''I Hakase''' (еҢ»еҚҡеЈ«) was an official position under the [[:en:RitsuryЕҚ|RitsuryЕҚ system]], belonging to the {{ill|Bureau of Pharmacy|ja|е…ёи–¬еҜ®|1=Bureau of Pharmacy|2=ja|3=е…ёи–¬еҜ®|4=fr|5=Bureau de pharmacie|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bureau of Pharmacy|13=User:Immanuelle/Bureau of Pharmacy|qq=}} within the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ (еҫӢд»ӨеҲ¶)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|qq=|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125558338}}.

== Overview ==

The term "I Hakase" first appears in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in entries for the 14th and 15th years of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]. These references, however, pertain to doctors sent as tribute from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] and are unrelated to Japan's own administrative system. By the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ([[:en:675|675]]), the term "Outer Pharmacy Bureau" (еӨ–и–¬еҜ®), later known as the Bureau of Medicine, is documented. It is inferred that the administrative structure equivalent to the Bureau of Medicine had already been established during the reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress JitЕҚ.

The position of I Hakase was held by a single appointee with an equivalent Rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|жӯЈдёғдҪҚдёӢ|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to жӯЈдёғдҪҚ}}, making it the highest-Ranking post among the various specialists within the Bureau of Medicine. Among the ten physicians employed, one with superior knowledge and experience was selected as I Hakase. The primary duties involved teaching various {{ill|medical prescriptions|ja|и–¬ж–№|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical prescriptions|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (yakuhЕҚ) and {{ill|pulse diagnosis texts|ja|и„ҲзөҢ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pulse diagnosis texts|qq=|1=и„Ҳз¶“|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612192}} (myakugyЕҚ) to the medical practitioners (еҢ»з”ҹ). Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onna I Hakase|qq=|1=Onna I Hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97184274}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.

In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official salary|qq=|1=official salary|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381656}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasonal salary|qq=|1=Kiroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448057}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, [[:en:791|791]], the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land allocated for official use|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҒ·еҲҶз”°|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|qq=|1=Ruiju Sandaikyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665185}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>

== References ==

{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==
* "I Hakase," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|еӣҪеҸІеӨ§иҫһе…ё|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}, Volume 1, p. 769, by Imae Hiromichi, Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan, 1979.

Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.

In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official salary|1=official salary|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381656}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasonal salary|1=Kiroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448057}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, [[:en:791|791]], the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land allocated for official use|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҒ·еҲҶз”°|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|1=Ruiju Sandaikyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665185}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>

'''I Hakase''' (еҢ»еҚҡеЈ«) was an official position under the [[:en:RitsuryЕҚ|RitsuryЕҚ system]], belonging to the {{ill|Bureau of Pharmacy|ja|е…ёи–¬еҜ®|1=Bureau of Pharmacy|2=ja|3=е…ёи–¬еҜ®|4=fr|5=Bureau de pharmacie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bureau of Pharmacy|13=User:Immanuelle/Bureau of Pharmacy|qq=}} within the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ (еҫӢд»ӨеҲ¶)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125558338}}.

== Overview ==

The term "I Hakase" first appears in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in entries for the 14th and 15th years of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]. These references, however, pertain to doctors sent as tribute from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] and are unrelated to Japan's own administrative system. By the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ([[:en:675|675]]), the term "Outer Pharmacy Bureau" (еӨ–и–¬еҜ®), later known as the Bureau of Medicine, is documented. It is inferred that the administrative structure equivalent to the Bureau of Medicine had already been established during the reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress JitЕҚ.

The position of I Hakase was held by a single appointee with an equivalent Rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|жӯЈдёғдҪҚдёӢ|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to жӯЈдёғдҪҚ}}, making it the highest-Ranking post among the various specialists within the Bureau of Medicine. Among the ten physicians employed, one with superior knowledge and experience was selected as I Hakase. The primary duties involved teaching various {{ill|medical prescriptions|ja|и–¬ж–№|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical prescriptions|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (yakuhЕҚ) and {{ill|pulse diagnosis texts|ja|и„ҲзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pulse diagnosis texts|1=и„Ҳз¶“|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612192}} (myakugyЕҚ) to the medical practitioners (еҢ»з”ҹ). Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onna I Hakase|1=Onna I Hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97184274}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.

In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official salary|1=official salary|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381656}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasonal salary|1=Kiroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448057}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, [[:en:791|791]], the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land allocated for official use|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҒ·еҲҶз”°|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|1=Ruiju Sandaikyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665185}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>

The position of I Hakase was held by a single appointee with an equivalent Rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|жӯЈдёғдҪҚдёӢ|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to жӯЈдёғдҪҚ}}, making it the highest-Ranking post among the various specialists within the Bureau of Medicine. Among the ten physicians employed, one with superior knowledge and experience was selected as I Hakase. The primary duties involved teaching various {{ill|medical prescriptions|ja|и–¬ж–№|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical prescriptions|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (yakuhЕҚ) and {{ill|pulse diagnosis texts|ja|и„ҲзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pulse diagnosis texts|1=и„Ҳз¶“|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612192}} (myakugyЕҚ) to the medical practitioners (еҢ»з”ҹ). Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onna I Hakase|1=Onna I Hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97184274}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.

In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official salary|1=official salary|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381656}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasonal salary|1=Kiroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448057}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, [[:en:791|791]], the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land allocated for official use|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҒ·еҲҶз”°|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|1=Ruiju Sandaikyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665185}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>

Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onna I Hakase|1=Onna I Hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97184274}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.

In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official salary|1=official salary|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381656}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasonal salary|1=Kiroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448057}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. Wikidata name: (none)
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|1=I Hakase is too ambiguous in english so using Medical Hakase as the name[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 19:15, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}

'''I Hakase''' (еҢ»еҚҡеЈ«) was an official position under the [[:en:RitsuryЕҚ|RitsuryЕҚ system]], belonging to the {{ill|Bureau of Pharmacy|ja|е…ёи–¬еҜ®|1=Bureau of Pharmacy|2=ja|3=е…ёи–¬еҜ®|4=fr|5=Bureau de pharmacie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bureau of Pharmacy|13=User:Immanuelle/Bureau of Pharmacy|qq=}} within the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ (еҫӢд»ӨеҲ¶)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125558338}}.

== Overview ==

The term "I Hakase" first appears in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in entries for the 14th and 15th years of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]. These references, however, pertain to doctors sent as tribute from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] and are unrelated to Japan's own administrative system. By the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ([[:en:675|675]]), the term "Outer Pharmacy Bureau" (еӨ–и–¬еҜ®), later known as the Bureau of Medicine, is documented. It is inferred that the administrative structure equivalent to the Bureau of Medicine had already been established during the reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress JitЕҚ.

The position of I Hakase was held by a single appointee with an equivalent Rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|жӯЈдёғдҪҚдёӢ|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to жӯЈдёғдҪҚ}}, making it the highest-Ranking post among the various specialists within the Bureau of Medicine. Among the ten physicians employed, one with superior knowledge and experience was selected as I Hakase. The primary duties involved teaching various {{ill|medical prescriptions|ja|и–¬ж–№|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical prescriptions|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (yakuhЕҚ) and {{ill|pulse diagnosis texts|ja|и„ҲзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pulse diagnosis texts|1=и„Ҳз¶“|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612192}} (myakugyЕҚ) to the medical practitioners (еҢ»з”ҹ). Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onna I Hakase|1=Onna I Hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97184274}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.

In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official salary|1=official salary|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381656}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasonal salary|1=Kiroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448057}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, [[:en:791|791]], the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land allocated for official use|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҒ·еҲҶз”°|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|1=Ruiju Sandaikyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665185}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>

== References ==

{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==
* "I Hakase," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|еӣҪеҸІеӨ§иҫһе…ё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}, Volume 1, p. 769, by Imae Hiromichi, Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan, 1979.

In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official salary|1=official salary|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381656}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasonal salary|1=Kiroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448057}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, [[:en:791|791]], the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land allocated for official use|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҒ·еҲҶз”°|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|1=Ruiju Sandaikyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665185}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "I Hakase," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|еӣҪеҸІеӨ§иҫһе…ё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}, Volume 1, p. 769, by Imae Hiromichi, Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan, 1979.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:еҢ»еҚҡеЈ«]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97310407]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I Hakase is too ambiguous in english so using Medical Hakase as the name[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 19:15, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''I Hakase''' (еҢ»еҚҡеЈ«) was an official position under the [[:en:RitsuryЕҚ|RitsuryЕҚ system]], belonging to the {{ill|Bureau of Pharmacy|ja|е…ёи–¬еҜ®|1=Bureau of Pharmacy|2=ja|3=е…ёи–¬еҜ®|4=fr|5=Bureau de pharmacie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bureau of Pharmacy|13=User:Immanuelle/Bureau of Pharmacy|qq=}} within the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ (еҫӢд»ӨеҲ¶)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125558338}}.
== Overview ==
The term "I Hakase" first appears in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in entries for the 14th and 15th years of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]. These references, however, pertain to doctors sent as tribute from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] and are unrelated to Japan's own administrative system. By the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ([[:en:675|675]]), the term "Outer Pharmacy Bureau" (еӨ–и–¬еҜ®), later known as the Bureau of Medicine, is documented. It is inferred that the administrative structure equivalent to the Bureau of Medicine had already been established during the reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress JitЕҚ.
The position of I Hakase was held by a single appointee with an equivalent Rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|жӯЈдёғдҪҚдёӢ|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to жӯЈдёғдҪҚ}}, making it the highest-Ranking post among the various specialists within the Bureau of Medicine. Among the ten physicians employed, one with superior knowledge and experience was selected as I Hakase. The primary duties involved teaching various {{ill|medical prescriptions|ja|и–¬ж–№|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical prescriptions|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (yakuhЕҚ) and {{ill|pulse diagnosis texts|ja|и„ҲзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pulse diagnosis texts|1=и„Ҳз¶“|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612192}} (myakugyЕҚ) to the medical practitioners (еҢ»з”ҹ). Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onna I Hakase|1=Onna I Hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97184274}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.
In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official salary|1=official salary|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381656}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasonal salary|1=Kiroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448057}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, [[:en:791|791]], the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land allocated for official use|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҒ·еҲҶз”°|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|1=Ruiju Sandaikyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665185}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "I Hakase," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|еӣҪеҸІеӨ§иҫһе…ё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}, Volume 1, p. 769, by Imae Hiromichi, Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan, 1979.
== See Also == * {{ill|IdЕҚ|ja|еҢ»йҒ“|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/IdЕҚ|1=IdЕҚ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11404712}} * {{ill|I TokugyЕҚsei|ja|еҢ»еҫ—жҘӯз”ҹ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/I TokugyЕҚsei|1=I TokugyЕҚsei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97175209}}

By the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ([[:en:675|675]]), the term "Outer Pharmacy Bureau" (еӨ–и–¬еҜ®), later known as the Bureau of Medicine, is documented. It is inferred that the administrative structure equivalent to the Bureau of Medicine had already been established during the reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress JitЕҚ.
The position of I Hakase was held by a single appointee with an equivalent Rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|жӯЈдёғдҪҚдёӢ|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to жӯЈдёғдҪҚ}}, making it the highest-Ranking post among the various specialists within the Bureau of Medicine. Among the ten physicians employed, one with superior knowledge and experience was selected as I Hakase. The primary duties involved teaching various {{ill|medical prescriptions|ja|и–¬ж–№|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical prescriptions|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (yakuhЕҚ) and {{ill|pulse diagnosis texts|ja|и„ҲзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pulse diagnosis texts|1=и„Ҳз¶“|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612192}} (myakugyЕҚ) to the medical practitioners (еҢ»з”ҹ). Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onna I Hakase|1=Onna I Hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97184274}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.
In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official salary|1=official salary|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381656}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasonal salary|1=Kiroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448057}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, [[:en:791|791]], the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land allocated for official use|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҒ·еҲҶз”°|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|1=Ruiju Sandaikyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665185}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:еҢ»еҚҡеЈ«]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:еҢ»еҚҡеЈ«]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:еҢ»еҚҡеЈ«]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97310407]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I Hakase is too ambiguous in english so using Medical Hakase as the name[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 19:15, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''I Hakase''' (еҢ»еҚҡеЈ«) was an official position under the [[:en:RitsuryЕҚ|RitsuryЕҚ system]], belonging to the {{ill|Bureau of Pharmacy|ja|е…ёи–¬еҜ®|1=Bureau of Pharmacy|2=ja|3=е…ёи–¬еҜ®|4=fr|5=Bureau de pharmacie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bureau of Pharmacy|13=User:Immanuelle/Bureau of Pharmacy|qq=}} within the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ (еҫӢд»ӨеҲ¶)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125558338}}.
== Overview ==
The term "I Hakase" first appears in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in entries for the 14th and 15th years of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]. These references, however, pertain to doctors sent as tribute from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] and are unrelated to Japan's own administrative system. By the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ([[:en:675|675]]), the term "Outer Pharmacy Bureau" (еӨ–и–¬еҜ®), later known as the Bureau of Medicine, is documented. It is inferred that the administrative structure equivalent to the Bureau of Medicine had already been established during the reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress JitЕҚ.
The position of I Hakase was held by a single appointee with an equivalent Rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|жӯЈдёғдҪҚдёӢ|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to жӯЈдёғдҪҚ}}, making it the highest-Ranking post among the various specialists within the Bureau of Medicine. Among the ten physicians employed, one with superior knowledge and experience was selected as I Hakase. The primary duties involved teaching various {{ill|medical prescriptions|ja|и–¬ж–№|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical prescriptions|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (yakuhЕҚ) and {{ill|pulse diagnosis texts|ja|и„ҲзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pulse diagnosis texts|1=и„Ҳз¶“|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612192}} (myakugyЕҚ) to the medical practitioners (еҢ»з”ҹ). Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onna I Hakase|1=Onna I Hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97184274}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.
In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official salary|1=official salary|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381656}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasonal salary|1=Kiroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448057}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, [[:en:791|791]], the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land allocated for official use|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҒ·еҲҶз”°|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|1=Ruiju Sandaikyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665185}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>

According to a decree issued on February 18, [[:en:791|791]], the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land allocated for official use|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҒ·еҲҶз”°|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|1=Ruiju Sandaikyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665185}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:еҢ»еҚҡеЈ«]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:еҢ»еҚҡеЈ«]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:еҢ»еҚҡеЈ«]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q97310407]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I Hakase is too ambiguous in english so using Medical Hakase as the name[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 19:15, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''I Hakase''' (еҢ»еҚҡеЈ«) was an official position under the [[:en:RitsuryЕҚ|RitsuryЕҚ system]], belonging to the {{ill|Bureau of Pharmacy|ja|е…ёи–¬еҜ®|1=Bureau of Pharmacy|2=ja|3=е…ёи–¬еҜ®|4=fr|5=Bureau de pharmacie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bureau of Pharmacy|13=User:Immanuelle/Bureau of Pharmacy|qq=}} within the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ (еҫӢд»ӨеҲ¶)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125558338}}.
== Overview ==
The term "I Hakase" first appears in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in entries for the 14th and 15th years of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]]. These references, however, pertain to doctors sent as tribute from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] and are unrelated to Japan's own administrative system. By the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ([[:en:675|675]]), the term "Outer Pharmacy Bureau" (еӨ–и–¬еҜ®), later known as the Bureau of Medicine, is documented. It is inferred that the administrative structure equivalent to the Bureau of Medicine had already been established during the reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress JitЕҚ.
The position of I Hakase was held by a single appointee with an equivalent Rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|жӯЈдёғдҪҚдёӢ|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to жӯЈдёғдҪҚ}}, making it the highest-Ranking post among the various specialists within the Bureau of Medicine. Among the ten physicians employed, one with superior knowledge and experience was selected as I Hakase. The primary duties involved teaching various {{ill|medical prescriptions|ja|и–¬ж–№|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical prescriptions|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (yakuhЕҚ) and {{ill|pulse diagnosis texts|ja|и„ҲзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pulse diagnosis texts|1=и„Ҳз¶“|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612192}} (myakugyЕҚ) to the medical practitioners (еҢ»з”ҹ). Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onna I Hakase|1=Onna I Hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97184274}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.
In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official salary|1=official salary|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381656}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasonal salary|1=Kiroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448057}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, [[:en:791|791]], the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land allocated for official use|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҒ·еҲҶз”°|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|1=Ruiju Sandaikyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665185}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>
== References ==

== Further Reading == * "I Hakase," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|еӣҪеҸІеӨ§иҫһе…ё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}, Volume 1, p. 769, by Imae Hiromichi, Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan, 1979.

By the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ({{ill|675|en|675|WD=Q30523|lt=675}}), the term "Outer Pharmacy Bureau" (еӨ–и–¬еҜ®), later known as the Bureau of Medicine, is documented. It is inferred that the administrative structure equivalent to the Bureau of Medicine had already been established during the reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress JitЕҚ.
The position of I Hakase was held by a single appointee with an equivalent Rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|жӯЈдёғдҪҚдёӢ|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to жӯЈдёғдҪҚ}}, making it the highest-Ranking post among the various specialists within the Bureau of Medicine. Among the ten physicians employed, one with superior knowledge and experience was selected as I Hakase. The primary duties involved teaching various {{ill|medical prescriptions|ja|и–¬ж–№|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical prescriptions|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (yakuhЕҚ) and {{ill|pulse diagnosis texts|ja|и„ҲзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pulse diagnosis texts|1=и„Ҳз¶“|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612192}} (myakugyЕҚ) to the medical practitioners (еҢ»з”ҹ). Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onna I Hakase|1=Onna I Hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97184274}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.
In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official salary|1=official salary|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381656}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasonal salary|1=Kiroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448057}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, {{ill|791|en|791|WD=Q31078|lt=791}}, the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land allocated for official use|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҒ·еҲҶз”°|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|1=Ruiju Sandaikyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665185}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>

Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.

'''I Hakase''' (еҢ»еҚҡеЈ«) was an official position under the {{ill|RitsuryЕҚ|en|RitsuryЕҚ|WD=Q742460|lt=RitsuryЕҚ system|1=RitsuryЕҚ}}, belonging to the {{ill|Bureau of Pharmacy|ja|е…ёи–¬еҜ®|1=Bureau of Pharmacy|2=ja|3=е…ёи–¬еҜ®|4=fr|5=Bureau de pharmacie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bureau of Pharmacy|13=User:Immanuelle/Bureau of Pharmacy|qq=}} within the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|ja|е®®еҶ…зңҒ (еҫӢд»ӨеҲ¶)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household (RitsuryЕҚ system)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q125558338}}.
== Overview ==
The term "I Hakase" first appears in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} in entries for the 14th and 15th years of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|en|Emperor Kinmei|WD=Q329792|lt=Emperor Kinmei|1=Emperor Kinmei}}. These references, however, pertain to doctors sent as tribute from [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] and are unrelated to Japan's own administrative system. By the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=еӨ©жӯҰеӨ©зҡҮ|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ({{ill|675|en|675|WD=Q30523|lt=675|1=675}}), the term "Outer Pharmacy Bureau" (еӨ–и–¬еҜ®), later known as the Bureau of Medicine, is documented. It is inferred that the administrative structure equivalent to the Bureau of Medicine had already been established during the reigns of Emperor Tenmu and Empress JitЕҚ.
The position of I Hakase was held by a single appointee with an equivalent Rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|жӯЈдёғдҪҚдёӢ|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to жӯЈдёғдҪҚ}}, making it the highest-Ranking post among the various specialists within the Bureau of Medicine. Among the ten physicians employed, one with superior knowledge and experience was selected as I Hakase. The primary duties involved teaching various {{ill|medical prescriptions|ja|и–¬ж–№|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical prescriptions|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (yakuhЕҚ) and {{ill|pulse diagnosis texts|ja|и„ҲзөҢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pulse diagnosis texts|1=и„Ҳз¶“|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612192}} (myakugyЕҚ) to the medical practitioners (еҢ»з”ҹ). Until the establishment of the position of {{ill|Onna I Hakase|ja|еҘіеҢ»еҚҡеЈ«|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onna I Hakase|1=Q97184274|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97184274}}, the I Hakase was also responsible for orally teaching obstetrics to 30 female physicians recruited from among official maidservants. Both male and female physicians were required to undergo monthly examinations.
In addition to {{ill|official salary|ja|дҪҚзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/official salary|1=Q11381656|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381656}} and {{ill|seasonal salary|ja|еӯЈзҰ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/seasonal salary|1=Kiroku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448057}}, the I Hakase received a one-time fee (жқҹи„©) of one piece of cloth from each entering medical student. According to a decree issued on February 18, {{ill|791|en|791|WD=Q31078|lt=791|1=791}}, the I Hakase was also granted four chЕҚ of {{ill|land allocated for official use|ja|иҒ·з”°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/land allocated for official use|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to иҒ·еҲҶз”°|1=Shokubunden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611232}}.<ref>гҖҺ{{ill|Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|ja|йЎһиҒҡдёүд»Јж ј|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruisan Sandai Kyaku|1=RuijЕ« Sandai Kyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665185}}гҖҸ, Volume 15, "Provisions for Official Land, Rank Land, and Administrative Land."</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * "I Hakase," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|еӣҪеҸІеӨ§иҫһе…ё|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=National History Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}, Volume 1, p. 769, by Imae Hiromichi, Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan, 1979.
